channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Jeremy Strong and Sebastian Stan on 'The Apprentice': 'We're way out on a limb'
item.description: Even in an election year, most seem to agree on one aspect about Ali Abbasi’s much-debated Donald Trump film “The Apprentice”: Sebastian Stan is a remarkably good Trump and Jeremy Strong is chillingly riveting as the New York power broker Roy Cohn.
item.dc:creator: Jake Coyle, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Former law enforcement officers, military vets step up to save stranded hurricane survivors without FEMA
item.description: A group of civilians has banded together to help with disaster relief in North Carolina in the aftermath of Hurricane Helene, which devastated several areas of the state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Social Security recipients will get a 2.5% cost-of-living boost in 2025, smaller than in recent past
item.description: Millions of retirees who receive Social Security benefits will see a 2.5% cost-of-living increase to their monthly checks beginning in January.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump's small-dollar donor fundraising is beset by confusion and fatigue
item.description: Donald Trump’s contributions from small-dollar donors have plummeted since his last White House campaign, presenting the former president with a financial challenge as he tries to keep pace with the Democrats’ fundraising machine.
item.dc:creator: Dan Merica, Aaron Kessler And Richard Lardner, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Harris faces new urgency to explain how her potential presidency would be different from Biden's
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — With less than four weeks until Election Day, Vice President Kamala Harris is facing new urgency to define how her potential presidency would be different from that of President Joe Biden.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A federal judge will hear more evidence on whether to reopen voter registration in Georgia
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — At least for now, a federal judge won’t order the state of Georgia to reopen voter registration for November’s elections. U.S.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US inflation reaches lowest point in 3 years, though some price pressures remain
item.description: Inflation in the United States dropped last month to its lowest point since it first began surging more than three years ago, adding to a spate of encouraging economic news in the closing weeks of the presidential race.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Rugaber, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Fox News Politics: Is Harris or Trump more accessible?
item.description: The latest updates from the 2024 campaign trail, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court appears split on Oklahoma death row inmate’s fate
item.description: The Supreme Court appeared split over an Oklahoma death row inmate’s latest bid for freedom during arguments Wednesday, leaving his fate uncertain. Richard Glossip was convicted and sentenced to death for the 1997 murder of his former boss, motel owner Barry Van Treese.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Social Security's scheduled cost of living increase 'won't make a dent' for some retirees
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Sherri Myers, an 82-year-old resident of Pensacola City, Florida, says the Social Security cost-of-living increase she'll receive in January “won't make a dent” in helping her meet her day-to-day expenses. “Inflation has eaten up my savings," she said.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Sen. Tim Johnson, the last Democrat to hold statewide office in South Dakota, dies at 77
item.description: SIOUX FALLS, S.D. (AP) — Former Sen. Tim Johnson, a centrist who was the last Democrat to hold statewide office in South Dakota and who was adept at securing federal funding for projects back home during his nearly three decades in Washington, has died. He was 77.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lake blames Gallego for border woes, he vows to protect abortion rights in Arizona Senate debate
item.description: Republican Senate candidate Kari Lake repeatedly reminded debate viewers of her three-decade career on Arizona television, looking to harness the connection she built with voters and overcome Democrat Ruben Gallego’s portrayal of her as a liar.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: In battleground states, it's not just about winning — but also losing by less
item.description: In a state where every vote matters, both Democratic and Republican campaigns are not only trying to win in counties where they’re strongest, they’re also trying to lose by less.
item.dc:creator: Ari Shapiro, Mia Venkat, Karen Zamora, and Ashley Brown, MPR News

item.title: How Hurricane Helene may affect the election in North Carolina
item.description: None of the 13 affected counties in North Carolina have a fully operational elections board, according to the state's Secretary of State office.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: WATCH: Candidates for Las Vegas Mayor face off in a debate ahead of Election Day
item.description: Las Vegas Mayoral candidates Shelley Berkley and Victoria Seaman will face off in a debate on Tuesday, hosted by 8 News Now, with the winner taking over the non-partisan office from Oscar Goodman, who served as mayor from 1999 to 2011.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, NewsNation

item.title: Lombardo among GOP governors criticizing Biden-Harris immigration asylum actions
item.description: Gov. Joe Lombardo was among 25 Republican governors who signed a letter criticizing the Biden-Harris administration over asylum policies that have brought more than a half-million immigrants into the United States.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Conservative senator lays out proposals for post-McConnell reforms
item.description: Sen. Mike Lee (R-Utah), the chair of the conservative Senate Steering Committee, has laid out a list of proposed reforms designed to ensure that the next Senate Republican leader does not have the same power as outgoing Senate GOP Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.).
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Moreno looks to close the gap on Sherrod Brown in Ohio
item.description: Bernie Moreno and Republicans are attempting to narrow the gap against Sen. Sherrod Brown (D) in Ohio as they try to nab one of the most contested races in the country in a battle for the Senate majority.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Harris pressed on changing positions, economic plan in '60 Minutes' interview
item.description: Vice President Harris was pressed on her changing policy positions and her expensive economic plan on “60 Minutes,” in what proved to be her toughest media interview since becoming the Democratic nominee.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Harris is pressed for more specifics in a wide-ranging interview with '60 Minutes'
item.description: The interview followed criticism from former President Donald Trump that Kamala Harris has been unwilling to sit for hard interviews. She was pressed on everything from her economic proposals to immigration.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: NJ senior property tax relief bill clears hurdle
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) - A bill in New Jersey tied to a program designed to cut seniors' property taxes in half cleared another hurdle in Trenton. If it's implemented, StayNJ would give a property tax break of up to $6,500 to seniors 65 and older making under half a million dollars a year.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Rafael Arroyo discusses wasteful spending, studio tax credits, and California's policies in Nevada
item.description: Rafael Arroyo, the Republican candidate running for Assembly District 41, joined Brian Loftis on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide, outlining his vision and priorities as the election approaches.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pro-Trump Christian nationalists are on tour to recruit election workers
item.description: “Are you guys ready to show the world that Christians will be silent no more?” said one speaker, whose nonprofit is recruiting people to become election workers.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Hagen and Audrey Nguyen, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court won't hear arguments in Texas emergency abortion case
item.description: The Supreme Court decided not to hear arguments in a case involving Texas that could have provided an answer about whether a state abortion ban conflicts with a federal emergency care law.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: Trump intends to use military to round up opponents: Michael Cohen
item.description: “The big warning that I want people to understand is when Donald Trump says something, stop sane washing it. Stop trying to make it into something which has some normalcy to it. What he says, he intends to do.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: This year’s Electoral College map may show another ‘blue shift.’ Here’s why
item.description: Differences in how quickly each state’s counties can report this year’s election results may lead to another “blue shift” or “red mirage” on the presidential electoral map.
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang, MPR News

item.title: Hurricane Milton projected to reach Category 4 strength before landfall in Florida
item.description: A new storm is approaching the west coast of the Florida, threatening to inflict heavy rain, strong winds and flash flooding in the already-storm battered state.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News
